name: Abdullah ibn Hasan
title: The Last Sacrifice
shortDescription: The young child of Imam Hasan (AS) who rushed to the battlefield in the final moments and used his arm as a shield for his uncle.
fullStory:
Abdullah ibn al-Hasan (AS) was the eleven-year-old child and legacy of Imam Hasan al-Mujtaba (AS), present in the tents with his mother on Ashura. Despite his young age, he had a profound understanding of the Imamate of his uncle Hussain (AS) and witnessed the tragic martyrdom of the companions and Bani Hashem throughout the day.

According to Sheikh Mufid's precise report in Al-Irshad, when Imam Hussain (AS) fell to the ground covered in blood and the enemy forces attacked him to finish him off, the young Abdullah could not remain indifferent. He ran out of the tents. Lady Zaynab (SA) tried to stop him to protect his life, but Abdullah resisted and cried out: 'By God, I will not separate from my uncle'.

He ran to the place of slaughter and stood beside his uncle. At that moment, Abjar ibn Ka'b (in some sources Bahr ibn Ka'b) raised his sword to strike Imam Hussain (AS). Young Abdullah, with unparalleled bravery, shouted: 'O son of an impure woman! Are you going to kill my uncle?'

Abdullah used his small arm as a shield against Abjar's sword. The sword fell, severing the child's arm, leaving it hanging only by the skin. Abdullah cried out in intense pain: 'O uncle!'. Imam Hussain (AS), though heavily wounded himself, embraced his nephew and said: 'O my nephew, be patient with what has befallen you and count it as good, for God will join you with your righteous forefathers'.

While Abdullah was resting in his uncle's embrace, Harmala ibn Kahil al-Asadi (the killer of Ali Asghar) shot an arrow, martyring this young child in the Imam's arms. Abdullah's sacrifice in the final minutes of Ashura displayed the peak of insight and love in the household of revelation.
quote: By God, I will never separate from my uncle.
